description.value:
Trigger A creature that is not a worshiper of Urgathoa touches the north door or the eastern double doors.

Effect The painted skeletons strike out of the mural: scythe +20 vs. each creature within 5 feet of any of the warded doors, @Damage[2d10+9[slashing]] (deadly — a critical hit also leaves the target @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1}). The skulls then exhale ungol dust through the whole of G1: each breathing creature there attempts a @Check[type:fortitude|dc:26], taking @Damage[2d6[poison]] and becoming @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1} on a failure (Drained 2 on a critical failure). The gas disperses after 1 round.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)

stealth.details: Trained — the painted scythe-skeletons' arms are subtly raised off the plaster.

details.description:
Potent Urgathoan wards seal the north door and the eastern double doors of the temple's entry hall (G1). The skeletons painted around them animate to reap any non-Urgathoan who touches either door, and their painted mouths vent ungol-dust gas into the room.

Linked alarm: triggering the doors also sets off an alarm in G8, alerting every creature in G8–G13 (see The Temple Under Alert).

Bypass: if a door is bathed in at least 10 points of vitality/positive energy (a channeled 3-action heal, for example) without anyone touching it, the ward is neutralized for 1 minute before it re-arms.
details.disable: @Check[type:thievery|dc:28] (expert) to scribe out the wards on a door without touching it, or dispel magic (4th rank, counteract DC 26) against each warded door.

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
